Alan Kent Haruf
(1943–2014) was an American writer born and raised in
Colorado
. He wrote six novels and several short stories set on the
High Plains
. As a young man Haruf enrolled in the
Peace Corps
in
lieu of military service
before receiving a master's degree from the
University of Iowa
. He initially struggled to establish a career as a writer. Haruf spent years teaching English at a high school and at universities. His writing was first published in 1984 when he was 41. Commercial success eluded him until the publication of
Plainsong
in 1999, which became a bestseller. Throughout Haruf's career, critics praised his spare and elegant prose, authentic portrayals of rural life, and attention to the beauty found in ordinary things, although he was occasionally criticized for redundancy. A Colorado magazine,
5280
, wrote that Haruf is "widely considered Colorado's finest novelist", and the
Dublin Review of Books
called his work "both uniquely American and profoundly universal".

The
orbicular batfish
(
Platax orbicularis
) is a species of marine
ray-finned fish
belonging to the family
Ephippidae
, the spadefishes and batfishes. It is native to the Indo-Pacific region, ranging from eastern Africa (including South Africa and Madagascar) eastwards to southern Japan and Australia, and in the Red Sea and the Persian Gulf. The species has also been recorded outside its native range in the western Atlantic Ocean. It inhabits shallow, protected coastal waters, including mangroves, coral reefs, and deeper areas with silty substrates. Juveniles typically shelter among mangrove roots and in protected lagoons. The orbicular batfish has a deep, highly compressed, round body, and adults can grow to more than 40 centimetres (16 in) in length. Larger individuals develop a slightly indented snout and a bony intraorbital protuberance. Adults are silvery grey in colour, with black vertical bars running through the eye and across the base of the pectoral fin. The dorsal and anal fins are yellowish with black margins. This orbicular batfish was photographed in the Red Sea off the coast of Egypt.

Alan Kent Haruf
(1943–2014) was an American writer born and raised in
Colorado
. He wrote six novels and several short stories set on the
High Plains
. As a young man Haruf enrolled in the
Peace Corps
in
lieu of military service
before receiving a master's degree from the
University of Iowa
. He initially struggled to establish a career as a writer. Haruf spent years teaching English at a high school and at universities. His writing was first published in 1984 when he was 41. Commercial success eluded him until the publication of
Plainsong
in 1999, which became a bestseller. Throughout Haruf's career, critics praised his spare and elegant prose, authentic portrayals of rural life, and attention to the beauty found in ordinary things, although he was occasionally criticized for redundancy. A Colorado magazine,
5280
, wrote that Haruf is "widely considered Colorado's finest novelist", and the
Dublin Review of Books
called his work "both uniquely American and profoundly universal".

The
orbicular batfish
(
Platax orbicularis
) is a species of marine
ray-finned fish
belonging to the family
Ephippidae
, the spadefishes and batfishes. It is native to the Indo-Pacific region, ranging from eastern Africa (including South Africa and Madagascar) eastwards to southern Japan and Australia, and in the Red Sea and the Persian Gulf. The species has also been recorded outside its native range in the western Atlantic Ocean. It inhabits shallow, protected coastal waters, including mangroves, coral reefs, and deeper areas with silty substrates. Juveniles typically shelter among mangrove roots and in protected lagoons. The orbicular batfish has a deep, highly compressed, round body, and adults can grow to more than 40 centimetres (16 in) in length. Larger individuals develop a slightly indented snout and a bony intraorbital protuberance. Adults are silvery grey in colour, with black vertical bars running through the eye and across the base of the pectoral fin. The dorsal and anal fins are yellowish with black margins. This orbicular batfish was photographed in the Red Sea off the coast of Egypt.

Diego Delso

Alan Kent Haruf
(1943–2014) was an American writer born and raised in
Colorado
. He wrote six novels and several short stories set on the
High Plains
. As a young man Haruf enrolled in the
Peace Corps
in
lieu of military service
before receiving a master's degree from the
University of Iowa
. He initially struggled to establish a career as a writer. Haruf spent years teaching English at a high school and at universities. His writing was first published in 1984 when he was 41. Commercial success eluded him until the publication of
Plainsong
in 1999, which became a bestseller. Throughout Haruf's career, critics praised his spare and elegant prose, authentic portrayals of rural life, and attention to the beauty found in ordinary things, although he was occasionally criticized for redundancy. A Colorado magazine,
5280
, wrote that Haruf is "widely considered Colorado's finest novelist", and the
Dublin Review of Books
called his work "both uniquely American and profoundly universal".

The
orbicular batfish
(
Platax orbicularis
) is a species of marine
ray-finned fish
belonging to the family
Ephippidae
, the spadefishes and batfishes. It is native to the Indo-Pacific region, ranging from eastern Africa (including South Africa and Madagascar) eastwards to southern Japan and Australia, and in the Red Sea and the Persian Gulf. The species has also been recorded outside its native range in the western Atlantic Ocean. It inhabits shallow, protected coastal waters, including mangroves, coral reefs, and deeper areas with silty substrates. Juveniles typically shelter among mangrove roots and in protected lagoons. The orbicular batfish has a deep, highly compressed, round body, and adults can grow to more than 40 centimetres (16 in) in length. 7341677b-b81f-4344-8b06-964f017c53c5.title: Sequential walk starting at Main Page (visit → extract → follow) — single-chain construction

details.intent: Build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs by repeatedly visiting a page, extracting an in-page link, then visiting that linked page. Produce the final ordered chain and one verified adjacency pair with an extracted evidence snippet showing the link on the source page pointing to the target page.
details.plan: 1) Visit the provided Main Page URL and extract candidate article links from the page content (e.g., featured article, in the news, or other article anchors). 2) Choose one link and visit its full page. 3) From that second page, extract a link to a third page; visit the third. 4) Repeat until chain length >= 4. 5) For one adjacency near the end (e.g., between chain[2] and chain[3]), show concrete evidence: when visiting chain[2], extract the HTML anchor or surrounding text that clearly links to chain[3] and include it as the verification snippet. 6) Return structured outputs: { chain: [URL0, URL1, URL2, URL3,...], adjacency_verification: { index: n, from_url, to_url, evidence_snippet } }.
measurable_output_fields: chain (ordered array of full HTTP(S) Wikipedia URLs, length >= 4)
adjacency_verification.index (integer, index i such that chain[i] links to chain[i+1])
adjacency_verification.evidence_snippet (string extracted from HTML/text on from_url showing the link)
details.why_this_reduces_uncertainty: A strict sequential visit ensures each chosen next URL is actually present on the previously visited page; visiting pages and extracting the anchor markup gives verifiable evidence that adjacency requirements are satisfied.
details.notes_about_benchmark_rule: Because a specific starting URL is given, we will visit it directly (no preliminary search). For subsequent pages we will visit each chosen link to extract and verify links. Every adjacency verification will be based on content extracted from a visit.
details.parent_goal:
Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

Alan Kent Haruf
(1943–2014) was an American writer born and raised in
Colorado
. He wrote six novels and several short stories set on the
High Plains
. As a young man Haruf enrolled in the
Peace Corps
in
lieu of military service
before receiving a master's degree from the
University of Iowa
. He initially struggled to establish a career as a writer. Haruf spent years teaching English at a high school and at universities. His writing was first published in 1984 when he was 41. Commercial success eluded him until the publication of
Plainsong
in 1999, which became a bestseller. Throughout Haruf's career, critics praised his spare and elegant prose, authentic portrayals of rural life, and attention to the beauty found in ordinary things, although he was occasionally criticized for redundancy. A Colorado magazine,
5280
, wrote that Haruf is "widely considered Colorado's finest novelist", and the
Dublin Review of Books
called his work "both uniquely American and profoundly universal".

Today's featured picture
The
orbicular batfish
(
Platax orbicularis
) is a species of marine
ray-finned fish
belonging to the family
Ephippidae
, the spadefishes and batfishes. It is native to the Indo-Pacific region, ranging from eastern Africa (including South Africa and Madagascar) eastwards to southern Japan and Australia, and in the Red Sea and the Persian Gulf. The species has also been recorded outside its native range in the western Atlantic Ocean. It inhabits shallow, protected coastal waters, including mangroves, coral reefs, and deeper areas with silty substrates. Juveniles typically shelter among mangrove roots and in protected lagoons. The orbicular batfish has a deep, highly compressed, round body, and adults can grow to more than 40 centimetres (16 in) in length. Larger individuals develop a slightly indented snout and a bony intraorbital protuberance. Adults are silvery grey in colour, with black vertical bars running through the eye and across the base of the pectoral fin. The dorsal and anal fins are yellowish with black margins. This orbicular batfish was photographed in the Red Sea off the coast of Egypt.

c33423cd-d297-4279-b2df-efb4286cc64b.title: Exploratory parallel testing from Main Page (visit main, extract several candidate links, then visit candidates in parallel to find a viable chain)

details.intent: Visit Main Page, extract a set of promising in-page article links (e.g., featured article, today's featured, selected content links). Then in parallel visit those candidate pages to discover links to additional pages, chaining until a 4+ URL chain is formed. Produce final chain and one verification snippet showing an adjacency.
details.plan: 1) Visit Main Page and extract a small set (3–6) of distinct article links present on the page. 2) In parallel, visit each candidate link and extract their outbound article links. 3) Choose and follow a sequence of links (using the parallel-extracted data to reduce traversal time) until chain length >= 4. 4) Select one adjacency near the end and extract the evidence snippet from the relevant visited page showing the link. 5) Return structured outputs as in candidate 1.
measurable_output_fields: initial_candidates (array of candidate URLs extracted from Main Page)
chain (ordered array of full HTTP(S) Wikipedia URLs, length >= 4)
details.why_this_reduces_uncertainty: Parallel visits of candidate pages speeds discovery of valid link transitions and provides multiple route options. Extracting anchors from visited pages supplies direct evidence for any claimed adjacency.
details.notes_about_benchmark_rule: We will still start by visiting the provided Main Page URL (no search). After extracting candidate links, subsequent page visits will be done in parallel to accelerate finding a valid chain, but every adjacency used will be verified by visiting the source page and extracting the link.
details.parent_goal:
Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

action_result.intent: Build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs by repeatedly visiting a page, extracting an in-page link, then visiting that linked page. Produce the final ordered chain and one verified adjacency pair with an extracted evidence snippet showing the link on the source page pointing to the target page.
vector_context: Sequential visits: start at Wikipedia Main Page and build a 4+ page chain (visit each page and extract links)

Alan Kent Haruf
(1943–2014) was an American writer born and raised in
Colorado
. He wrote six novels and several short stories set on the
High Plains
. As a young man Haruf enrolled in the
Peace Corps
in
lieu of military service
before receiving a master's degree from the
University of Iowa
. He initially struggled to establish a career as a writer. Haruf spent years teaching English at a high school and at universities. His writing was first published in 1984 when he was 41. Commercial success eluded him until the publication of
Plainsong
in 1999, which became a bestseller. Throughout Haruf's career, critics praised his spare and elegant prose, authentic portrayals of rural life, and attention to the beauty found in ordinary things, although he was occasionally criticized for redundancy. A Colorado magazine,
5280

The
orbicular batfish
(
Platax orbicularis
) is a species of marine
ray-finned fish
belonging to the family
Ephippidae
, the spadefishes and batfishes. It is native to the Indo-Pacific region, ranging from eastern Africa (including South Africa and Madagascar) eastwards to southern Japan and Australia, and in the Red Sea and the Persian Gulf. The species has also been recorded outside its native range in the western Atlantic Ocean. It inhabits shallow, protected coastal waters, including mangroves, coral reefs, and deeper areas with silty substrates. Juveniles typically shelter among mangrove roots and in protected lagoons. The orbicular batfish has a deep, highly compressed, round body, and adults can grow to more than 40 centimetres (16 in) in length. Larger individuals develop a slightly indented snout and a bony intraorbital protuberance. Adults are silvery grey in colour, with black vertical bars running through the eye and across the base of the pectoral fin. The dorsal and anal fins are yellowish with black margins. This orbicular batfish was photographed in the Red Sea off the coast of Egypt.

Yuma, Colorado
, served as an inspiration for Haruf's small-town settings.
Alan Kent Haruf
(1943–2014) was an American writer born and raised in
Colorado
. He wrote six novels and several short stories set on the
High Plains
. As a young man Haruf enrolled in the
Peace Corps
in
lieu of military service
before receiving a master's degree from the
University of Iowa
. He initially struggled to establish a career as a writer. Haruf spent years teaching English at a high school and at universities. His writing was first published in 1984 when he was 41. Commercial success eluded him until the publication of
Plainsong
in 1999, which became a bestseller. Throughout Haruf's career, critics praised his spare and elegant prose, authentic portrayals of rural life, and attention to the beauty found in ordinary things, although he was occasionally criticized for redundancy. A Colorado magazine,
5280
, wrote that Haruf is "widely considered Colorado's finest novelist", and the
Dublin Review of Books
called his work "both uniquely American and profoundly universal".

The
orbicular batfish
(
Platax orbicularis
) is a species of marine
ray-finned fish
belonging to the family
Ephippidae
, the spadefishes and batfishes. It is native to the Indo-Pacific region, ranging from eastern Africa (including South Africa and Madagascar) eastwards to southern Japan and Australia, and in the Red Sea and the Persian Gulf. The species has also been recorded outside its native range in the western Atlantic Ocean. It inhabits shallow, protected coastal waters, including mangroves, coral reefs, and deeper areas with silty substrates. Juveniles typically shelter among mangrove roots and in protected lagoons. The orbicular batfish has a deep, highly compressed, round body, and adults can grow to more than 40 centimetres (16 in) in length.
